spec_match.rationale: The page probes AudioContext.renderCapacity directly and uses start(), stop(), and update events only when that property exists.

interactive.rationale: Start, stop, add/remove oscillator, and update-interval controls were exercised; ordinary audio still runs when capacity monitoring is absent.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The monitor explains live average, peak, and underrun statistics without pretending unavailable values were collected.

edge_cases.rationale: Missing AudioContext and missing renderCapacity have separate, capability-specific states; repeated and out-of-order controls produce actionable log entries.

references.rationale: ChromeStatus feature 5172818344148992 and MDN AudioRenderCapacity are linked.

runtime_evidence.rationale: Headless Chrome 149 exposed AudioContext but not renderCapacity; playback, oscillator changes, and stop completed without console or network failures.

accessibility.rationale: Controls have native names, the interval slider is labelled, and changing outputs use output/status semantics.

design_system.rationale: Fallback messaging remains visually coherent and no longer tells a newer browser user to upgrade solely from a missing capability.

summary: Replaced browser-version inference and the false “upgrade to Chrome 146+” warning with exact AudioContext/renderCapacity capability reporting. Verified all controls and the unsupported-monitoring audio path.
